channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Spin-exciton coupling modified by interfacial magnetic interactions in a van der Waals heterostructure
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 10 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69389-x

Excitons are important excitations in solids with potential applications in quantum and spintronic technologies. The authors achieve bidirectional control of exciton energy in a twodimensional antiferromagnetic semiconductor via interfacial spin-exciton coupling.
item.dc:title: Spin-exciton coupling modified by interfacial magnetic interactions in a van der Waals heterostructure
item.dc:creator: Weican Lan
Chaocheng Liu
Yajuan Feng

Lu Cheng

Wensheng Yan

item.title: Publisher Correction: [Hg<sub>3</sub>Se<sub>2</sub>]<sup>2-</sup> cluster drives giant optical anisotropy and broad infrared transparency
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 09 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69406-z

Publisher Correction: [Hg3Se2]2- cluster drives giant optical anisotropy and broad infrared transparency
item.dc:title: Publisher Correction: [Hg<sub>3</sub>Se<sub>2</sub>]<sup>2-</sup> cluster drives giant optical anisotropy and broad infrared transparency
item.dc:creator: Qixian Ren
Chen Cui
Xinchen Chen
Yabo Wu
Ran An
Zhihua Yang
Shilie Pan

item.title: Phase engineering of relaxor ferroelectricity in van der Waals crystal
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 09 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69272-9

Relaxor ferroelectricity is achieved in two-dimensional CuInP2(S1−xSex)6 crystal via controlling the monoclinic and trigonal coexist phase, enabling memristors with more analog states and lower operating voltages.
item.dc:title: Phase engineering of relaxor ferroelectricity in van der Waals crystal
item.dc:creator: Tao Yang
Yinchang Ma
Dongxing Zheng
Weili Deng

Maolin Chen
